name: Formal front entrance
description: A grand, perfectly symmetric main-entry treatment for the principal facade of a civic, ceremonial or aristocratic building. The composition centres a single or double door between two flanking pilasters that rise the full storey height, crowned by a triangular or segmental pediment whose apex aligns with the building's central axis. A raised threshold step (one block of @primary or @accent) lifts the door above the ground plane to dignify arrival and shed water. Optional shallow porch or full portico may be added in front, sheltering the threshold and carrying the pediment forward on free-standing columns. Used for mansions, town halls, temples, courthouses and castle great-hall fronts where the entrance must read as the unmistakable centre of the elevation.

required_furniture.rule: Single or paired door at the exact centre of the bay; if double, hinges symmetric (left/right).
One flanking pilaster on each side of the door, full storey height, made of @accent block.
Triangular pediment of @accent stairs/slabs above the lintel, apex on the building's central axis.
One-block-high raised step of @primary or @accent immediately in front of the door, full door width.
Horizontal accent course spanning pilaster-to-pilaster directly above the door, base of the pediment.

placement_rules: Door is placed on the perfect centre line of the bay; offsets break the formal reading and are disallowed.
Pilasters are mirrored about the door axis with identical height and projection.
Pediment apex sits on the same vertical axis as the door centre.
Threshold step extends outward at least one block beyond the door footprint.
When a porch/portico is added, its columns align with (or sit forward of) the pilasters; the pediment then crowns the portico, not the wall.
This skill consumes the central bay of the front elevation: no windows or other apertures within the pilastered span.
lighting_ventilation: As the building's primary public face, the formal entrance is the dominant aperture of the front elevation; a pair of flanking lanterns or wall-mounted @light blocks on the pilasters at head height marks the threshold at night and satisfies Java 1.16.5 mob-spawn rules around the approach.
